evals.prompt: /remember — The user says: 'always use Pest for tests in this project, never PHPUnit'. Store this so future sessions inherit the rule.
assertions: The response writes a tier-2 topic file under `.claude/memory/topics/` with a slug like `testing-framework.md` (or similar) — per the three-tier architecture in SKILL.md.
The topic file body contains the rule (use Pest, not PHPUnit), a `Last verified:` date, and a `Version: v1` header per the SKILL.md template snippet.
The response also adds (or updates) a one-line pointer in `.claude/memory/index.md` referencing the new topic — tier-1 index is always-loaded per the SKILL.md table.
The response does NOT dump the rule into CLAUDE.md — memory is for decisions that don't fit a global rules file, per the SKILL.md `when_to_use` ('too subtle to live only in CLAUDE.md').

evals.prompt: /remember — A topic file already exists at `.claude/memory/topics/deploy-process.md` (v1). The user wants to update it: 'we now deploy via blue-green, not rolling.' Update the topic.

files.content:
# Deploy Process

Last verified: 2026-04-01
Version: v1
Previous: (none)

Deploys are rolling — instances replaced 25% at a time over 20 minutes.

assertions: The response writes a new version of the topic file (v2) with the updated content (blue-green) and updates the `Version:` header.
The `Previous:` field in the new version references the prior version (v1) — version chain preserved per the SKILL.md template.
The response also snapshots the prior content to the lineage ledger (`.claude/lineage/...`) per the SKILL.md description (`snapshots any prior version to the lineage ledger`).
The tier-1 pointer in `.claude/memory/index.md` is NOT duplicated — the existing pointer is reused, not re-added.

evals.prompt: User: 'remember to check the deploy logs after we ship today.' Should /remember store this?
assertions: The response declines to write a memory topic — this is an ephemeral session-scoped reminder, not a cross-session rule per the SKILL.md `when_to_use` exclusion ('Do NOT use for ... ephemeral session notes — those belong in TaskCreate or progress-log').
The response recommends TaskCreate for the immediate reminder OR /progress-log for capturing the post-deploy check at session end.
The response does NOT silently invoke /remember as a substitute — the skill is for durable rules, not in-session todos.
The response distinguishes 'remember to do X' (todo) from 'remember that Y' (rule), choosing the right tool per the verbal cue.
